papers.title_en: Convex Relaxations for the Optimization of Markov Processes
papers.authors: Hongyi Zhang, Yuehaw Khoo, Tianyun Tang
papers.evidence: 优化插值两个指定概率分布的马尔可夫过程，涉及动态最优传输
papers.tldr: 针对高维状态空间中马尔可夫过程优化面临的维数灾难，提出基于序列耦合的凸松弛方法。通过引入局部边际和簇矩约束，将原问题转化为可计算的下界问题，并恢复中间分布的低阶统计量。该方法将动态最优传输作为特例，可从松弛解中恢复Benamou-Brenier动力学。实验表明该框架可扩展到一般马尔可夫过程，如Ising模型间的约束插值。
papers.abstract_en: In this paper, we study the problem of optimizing Markov processes that interpolate between two prescribed probability distributions while minimizing a given cost. The main computational challenge is the curse of dimensionality: in high-dimensional state spaces, representing the full distribution is intractable. To address this, we reformulate the problem in terms of sequential couplings and develop convex relaxations based on local marginals and cluster moments. These relaxations exploit locality and sparse interaction structure, provide computable lower bounds, and recover low-order statistics of the intermediate laws. We identify dynamic optimal transport as a special case of our Markov process optimization problem and develop a procedure for recovering the underlying Benamou--Brenier dynamics from the relaxed solution. We also show that the procedure extends to more general Markov processes and illustrate it with a constrained process between Ising models.
